title: Islamia College University

[Higher Education Commission of Pakistan](/wiki/Higher_Education_Commission_of_Pakistan "Higher Education Commission of Pakistan")
[Higher Education Commission of Pakistan](/wiki/Higher_Education_Commission_of_Pakistan "Higher Education Commission of Pakistan")
[Prof. Dr Zia Ul Haq](/wiki/Zia_Ul_Haq_(vice_chancellor) "Zia Ul Haq (vice chancellor)")

(**ICP**) ([Urdu](/wiki/Urdu_language "Urdu language"): اسلامیہ کالج پشاور) is a public university located in [Peshawar, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa](/wiki/Peshawar,_Khyber_Pakhtunkhwa "Peshawar, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa"), Pakistan.[[1]](#cite_note-uniRank-1)[[2]](#cite_note-ICP_location-2)
Rs 100,000/- was sent by the prime minister of Amb [Syed Abdul Jabbar Shah](/wiki/Syed_Abdul_Jabbar_Shah "Syed Abdul Jabbar Shah") to Sahibzada Abdul Qayyum as donation for the college.

outgoing_links.text: Nawab Sir Muhammad Khan Zaman Khan Tanoli

**Islamia College University Peshawar** (**ICP**) ([Urdu](/wiki/Urdu_language "Urdu language"): اسلامیہ کالج پشاور) is a public university located in [Peshawar, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa](/wiki/Peshawar,_Khyber_Pakhtunkhwa "Peshawar, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa"), Pakistan.[[1]](#cite_note-uniRank-1)[[2]](#cite_note-ICP_location-2)

## Degrees awarded

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Islamia_College_University&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Degrees awarded")]

Initially established as a college, Islamia College Peshawar awarded higher secondary school certificates. It was later converted into a university and now also awards numerous undergraduate and postgraduate degrees in the fields of Arts & Humanities, Business & Social Sciences, Language & Cultural, Engineering and Science & Technology.[[1]](#cite_note-uniRank-1)

This university is ranked 24th on the List of Top Universities in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa in 2022.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Recognized university

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Islamia_College_University&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Recognized university")]

This university is recognized by the [Higher Education Commission of Pakistan](/wiki/Higher_Education_Commission_of_Pakistan "Higher Education Commission of Pakistan").[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[1]](#cite_note-uniRank-1)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Islamia_College_University&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: History")]

Founded by [Sir S.A. Qayyum](/wiki/Sahibzada_Abdul_Qayyum "Sahibzada Abdul Qayyum") and [Sir George Roos-Keppel](/wiki/George_Roos-Keppel "George Roos-Keppel") in 1913, it is one of the oldest institutions of [higher education in Pakistan](/wiki/Higher_education_in_Pakistan "Higher education in Pakistan"), and its historical roots are traced from the culminating point of the [Aligarh Movement](/wiki/Aligarh_Movement "Aligarh Movement").[[5]](#cite_note-History_of_Islamia_College_University-5) The university provides [higher learning](/wiki/Higher_education_in_Pakistan "Higher education in Pakistan") in [arts](/wiki/Arts "Arts"), [languages](/wiki/Languages "Languages"), [humanities](/wiki/Humanities "Humanities"), [social sciences](/wiki/Social_Sciences "Social Sciences") and modern [sciences](/wiki/Science "Science").[[5]](#cite_note-History_of_Islamia_College_University-5) In 1950, the [University of Peshawar](/wiki/University_of_Peshawar "University of Peshawar") was founded as an offshoot of Islamia College Peshawar, with the later being associated to the university as a constituent college.[[5]](#cite_note-History_of_Islamia_College_University-5) Initially established as Islamia College, it was granted university status by the [Government of Pakistan](/wiki/Government_of_Pakistan "Government of Pakistan") in 2008; the word college is retained in its title for preserving its historical roots.[[5]](#cite_note-History_of_Islamia_College_University-5)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/e/ec/1917_Darband%2C_SIR_GEORGE_ROOSE_KEPPEL%2C_SAHIBAZADA_SIR_ABDUL_QAYUM.jpg/250px-1917_Darband%2C_SIR_GEORGE_ROOSE_KEPPEL%2C_SAHIBAZADA_SIR_ABDUL_QAYUM.jpg)](/wiki/File:1917_Darband,_SIR_GEORGE_ROOSE_KEPPEL,_SAHIBAZADA_SIR_ABDUL_QAYUM.jpg)

This picture is from 1917, [Darband](/wiki/Darband,_Khyber_Pakhtunkhwa "Darband, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa"). In this photo: [Nawab Sir Muhammad Khan Zaman Khan Tanoli](/wiki/Muhammad_Khan_Zaman_Khan "Muhammad Khan Zaman Khan") (seated second from left), [Sir George Roos-Keppel](/wiki/George_Roos-Keppel "George Roos-Keppel") (seated third from left), Sahibzada Sir [Abdul Qayyum Khan](/wiki/Sahibzada_Abdul_Qayyum "Sahibzada Abdul Qayyum") (seated first from right) Thanking to [Nawab of Amb](/wiki/Amb_(princely_state) "Amb (princely state)") for huge amount of Cash donation.

### Cash Donations by Nawabs

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Islamia_College_University&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Cash Donations by Nawabs")]

On June 2, 1911, a delegation headed by Sahibzaba Abdul Qayyum Khan visited Tangi Charsadda and set up his camp at the Hujra of Khan Bahadur Ghulam Haider Khan of Tangi for collection of donations. Khans of Tangi donated a handsome amount of Rs.25000 and the delegation stayed for 3 days at Tangi and then moved to Umarzai village where donation of Rs.12000 was collected and then to Turangzai where Rs.20000 and from Rajar Rs.3000 and finally to village Charsadda. In total Rs 150,000 was collected from Hashtnagar. Subsequently, a large plot of land was purchased for the college building from the Khalil (Arbabs) of Tehkal and Sufaid Dheri Rs. 150,000/- from [Nizam of Hyderabad](/wiki/Nizam_of_Hyderabad "Nizam of Hyderabad") and the construction cost building Paid by **[Nawab of Amb](/wiki/Amb_(princely_state) "Amb (princely state)")** Rs 100,000/- was sent by the prime minister of Amb [Syed Abdul Jabbar Shah](/wiki/Syed_Abdul_Jabbar_Shah "Syed Abdul Jabbar Shah") to Sahibzada Abdul Qayyum as donation for the college.

### Conception

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Islamia_College_University&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Conception")]

When the new province of [Khyber Pakhtunkhwa](/wiki/Khyber_Pakhtunkhwa "Khyber Pakhtunkhwa") (previously known as N.W.F.P) was formed in 1901 after its separation from the [Punjab](/wiki/Punjab_(British_India) "Punjab (British India)"), there was only one college ([Edwardes College](/wiki/Edwardes_College "Edwardes College")) in whole province. This scarcity of quality educational institutions forced local youth to travel to faraway regions of the country ([British India](/wiki/British_Raj "British Raj")) in pursuit of higher education. This same lack of educational opportunities in the region motivated Nawab Sir Sahibzada Abdul Qayyum and Sir George Roos-Keppel to establish an institution that would not only cater to the academic needs of the region but also produce leaders from the region.

### Tribal Hostel ICP

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Islamia_College_University&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Tribal Hostel ICP")]

Founded by [Iskander Mirza](/wiki/Iskander_Mirza "Iskander Mirza") (former president of Pakistan) on 7 July 1956. The hostel system of [Islamia College University, Peshawar](/wiki/Islamia_College_University,_Peshawar "Islamia College University, Peshawar") exists since 1913. Tribal Hostel is located in the center of [Islamia College University, Peshawar](/wiki/Islamia_College_University,_Peshawar "Islamia College University, Peshawar").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/ab/Islamia_College_Peshawar_%28Public_Sector_University%29%2C_Khyber_Pakhtunkhwa%2C_Pakistan_cropped.jpg/250px-Islamia_College_Peshawar_%28Public_Sector_University%29%2C_Khyber_Pakhtunkhwa%2C_Pakistan_cropped.jpg)](/wiki/File:Islamia_College_Peshawar_(Public_Sector_University),_Khyber_Pakhtunkhwa,_Pakistan_cropped.jpg)

Islamia College Peshawar side view

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/df/Islamia_College_Peshawar_2.jpg/250px-Islamia_College_Peshawar_2.jpg)](/wiki/File:Islamia_College_Peshawar_2.jpg)

Aerial View of the College Grounds

### Foundation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Islamia_College_University&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Foundation")]

By 1909, as the idea of a college in the province was taking shape in the minds of both Nawab Sir [Sahibzada Abdul Qayyum](/wiki/Sahibzada_Abdul_Qayyum "Sahibzada Abdul Qayyum") and [Sir George Roos-Keppel](/wiki/Sir_George_Roos-Keppel "Sir George Roos-Keppel"), it was further strengthened by their visit to the [Aligarh Muslim University](/wiki/Aligarh_Muslim_University "Aligarh Muslim University") the same year. Nawab Sahib asked the students, especially those from the N.W.F.P. who were studying there as to what were the problems they were facing there and how he could help them. The students told him that they needed a hostel. Nawab Sahib informed them that rather than building them a hostel at Aligarh, he would build them a college at Peshawar. Maulana Qutabshah was the first dean of the college.

Consequently, on April 12, 1911, Nawab Sahib arranged a meeting of like minded people in [Peshawar](/wiki/Peshawar "Peshawar") at the residence of Abdul Karim, contractor at [Peshawar](/wiki/Peshawar "Peshawar") city, which was attended by Ghulam Haider Khan of Tangi Charsadda, Habibullah Khan, Khushal Khan, Sethi Karim Bakhsh, Sir [Sahibzada Abdul Qayyum](/wiki/Sahibzada_Abdul_Qayyum "Sahibzada Abdul Qayyum"), Khan Sahib [Abdul Majid Khan](/wiki/Abdul_Majid_Khan_Tarin "Abdul Majid Khan Tarin") and others. Sir [Sahibzada Abdul Qayyum](/wiki/Sahibzada_Abdul_Qayyum "Sahibzada Abdul Qayyum") moved the motion for collection of contributions, which was instantly responded to by cash donations by all present. Subsequently, a large plot of land was purchased for the college building from the Khalil (Arbabs) of Tehkal Rs. 150,000/- from [Nizam of Hyderabad](/wiki/Nizam_of_Hyderabad "Nizam of Hyderabad") and the construction cost building Paid by **[Nawab of Amb](/wiki/Amb_(princely_state) "Amb (princely state)")** Rs 100,000/- was sent by the prime minister of Amb [Syed Abdul Jabbar Shah](/wiki/Syed_Abdul_Jabbar_Shah "Syed Abdul Jabbar Shah") to Sahibzada Abdul Qayyum as donation for the college. Other chiefs and nobles of the North-West Frontier and Punjab, also made various donations.

[Haji Sahib of Turangzai](/wiki/Turangzai "Turangzai"), the most famous Pukhtun religious leader of the time was requested by Nawab Sir [Sahibzada Abdul Qayyum](/wiki/Sahibzada_Abdul_Qayyum "Sahibzada Abdul Qayyum") to lay the foundation stone of Islamia College. Haji Sahib agreed to the request, however, he had been declared a proclaimed offender by the British for his anti-British activities and his entry was banned into British controlled territory. He was residing in tribal territory, which was outside British control, so Nawab Sahib prevailed upon Sir [George Roos-Keppel](/wiki/George_Roos-Keppel "George Roos-Keppel") and the British to permit Haji Sahib to enter British controlled territory for one day so he could lay the foundation stone of Islamia College. The British agreed to this request with the understanding that Haji Sahib would return to tribal territory once he had laid the foundation stone. Haji Sahib was permitted to enter British controlled territory for the ceremony and spent the night in the 'Pokh' [Mosque](/wiki/Mosque "Mosque") of Tehkal. At the foundation stone laying ceremony, Sir [Roos Keppel](/wiki/George_Roos-Keppel "George Roos-Keppel") and other British officials were present, so Haji Sahib hid his face in his sheet (Chadar) from them and was led by Sheikh Muhammad Ibrahim to the place where he was to lay the foundation stone. After laying the stone Haji Sahib went to Tehkal and then returned to the tribal territory.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/30/Commemorative_coin_issued_by_the_State_Bank_of_Pakistan_on_the_Centenary_Celebrations_of_Islamia_College_Peshawar.jpg/330px-Commemorative_coin_issued_by_the_State_Bank_of_Pakistan_on_the_Centenary_Celebrations_of_Islamia_College_Peshawar.jpg)](/wiki/File:Commemorative_coin_issued_by_the_State_Bank_of_Pakistan_on_the_Centenary_Celebrations_of_Islamia_College_Peshawar.jpg)

Commemorative coin issued by the State Bank of Pakistan on the Centenary Celebrations of Islamia College Peshawar

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e0/Islamia_College_on_1000_Rupees_note.jpg/330px-Islamia_College_on_1000_Rupees_note.jpg)](/wiki/File:Islamia_College_on_1000_Rupees_note.jpg)

Islamia College on 1000 PKR Note

### Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah's will

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Islamia_College_University&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah's will")]

Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah visited this College in 1936, 1945 and 1948. Quaid-e-Azam, who became lifelong honorary member of the Khyber Union ICP in 1936, made the college one of the heirs of his property in 1939:

"All my residuary estate including the corpus that may fall after the lapse of life interest or otherwise to be divided into three parts - and I bequeath one part to Aligarh University - one part to Islamia College Peshawar and one part to Sindh Madrassa of Karachi..." - *Quaid-e-Azam's Will, Bombay, May 30, 1939*

Addressing the students of the college on April 12, 1948, he said: "Let me tell you that nothing is nearer to my heart than to have a great center of culture and learning in a place like Peshawar, a place from where the rays of knowledge and culture can spread throughout the Middle East and Central Asia."

## Endowment

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Islamia_College_University&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Endowment")]

According to the board of trustees, the college, which is spread over 300 acres of land, has 1089 Jaribs cultivable land in Harichand, Rai Killi and Tarnab, (District Charsadda). In addition to this, there are 395 shops and flats in the Khyber Bazaar Peshawar and main bazaar Charsadda.

## Notable alumni

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Islamia_College_University&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Notable alumni")]

The following is a list of notable alumni of Islamia College University, Peshawar, and its predecessor institution, Islamia College Peshawar:

* **[Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan](/wiki/Abdul_Ghaffar_Khan "Abdul Ghaffar Khan")** – Pashtun independence activist, political leader, and founder of the Khudai Khidmatgar movement.
* **[Abdul Rab Nishtar](/wiki/Abdur_Rab_Nishtar "Abdur Rab Nishtar")** – Pakistani statesman, close associate of Muhammad Ali Jinnah, and former Governor of Punjab.
* **[Inayatullah Khan Mashriqi](/wiki/Inayatullah_Khan_Mashriqi "Inayatullah Khan Mashriqi")** – Mathematician, Islamic scholar, and founder of the Khaksar Movement.
* **[Roedad Khan](/wiki/Roedad_Khan "Roedad Khan")** – Senior civil servant, author, and former Governor of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa and Sindh.
* **[Ajmal Khattak](/wiki/Ajmal_Khattak "Ajmal Khattak")** – Pashto poet, journalist, and political activist.
* **[Hamza Shinwari](/wiki/Hamza_Shinwari "Hamza Shinwari")** – Renowned Pashto poet and literary figure.
* **[Arif Nizami](/wiki/Arif_Nizami "Arif Nizami")** – Veteran journalist and former editor-in-chief of *The Nation* newspaper.
* **[Hayat Mohammad Khan Sherpao](/wiki/Hayat_Mohammad_Khan_Sherpao "Hayat Mohammad Khan Sherpao")** – Pakistani politician and former Governor of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa.
* **[Waqar Ahmed Seth](/wiki/Waqar_Ahmed_Seth "Waqar Ahmed Seth")** – Jurist and former Chief Justice of the Peshawar High Court.
